name: Oxford
description_short: Oxford Federal Credit Union is a Maine-based credit union established in 1938, serving over 19,400 members across a nine-county rural region with $318.1M in assets.
description_long:
Oxford Federal Credit Union was established in 1938 and is headquartered in Mexico, Maine. The credit union operates as a member-owned financial cooperative serving a defined geographic community across nine rural Maine counties: Androscoggin, Cumberland, Franklin, Kennebec, Knox, Lincoln, Oxford, Sagadahoc, Somerset, and York. With over 19,400 loyal members and approximately 39 dedicated employees, OFCU represents the 14th largest credit union in Maine and stewards over $318.1 million in assets.

Oxford Federal Credit Union offers a comprehensive range of banking services typical of full-service credit unions. These include deposit products (checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit), credit products (auto loans, personal loans, business loans, and mortgages), payment services (bill pay, online loan payments, mobile payments, and remote deposit capture), and digital services (online banking, mobile app, e-statements, and electronic signature authentication). The credit union also provides financial counseling, financial education, and student scholarships to support community members.

What distinguishes Oxford FCU is its tight geographic focus on a specific nine-county rural Maine region, which creates strong community ties and localized service. The credit union has maintained continuous operation since 1938, demonstrating institutional stability. Their membership eligibility extends to anyone living, working, worshiping, attending school, or conducting business in their service area, making them accessible to a broad segment of the rural Maine population. The credit union maintains physical presence through one main branch with 2 ATMs and offers both traditional and modern digital banking channels.

However, potential members should note several limitations. The credit union operates with a single physical branch location, which may be inconvenient for those outside the Mexico, Maine area. Service availability is currently limited—the website indicates the lobby is "currently closed," which may reflect operational constraints. Geographic membership restrictions mean only those meeting specific residency or employment criteria in the nine-county service area can join. With only 19,400 members and $318.1M in assets, OFCU is a smaller institution that may have more limited product customization or specialized services compared to larger regional credit unions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 13,600+ members)
Mortgage loans (home purchase, refinance, and other mortgage options)
Bill pay and online loan payments
pros: Established 1938 with 80+ years of operational history and institutional stability in rural Maine
Serves 19,400+ members across nine-county region with clear community focus and accessibility
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, auto loans, personal loans, business loans, and mortgages
Digital services fully operational including online banking, mobile app (iOS/Android), bill pay, and remote deposit capture
Member-owned cooperative structure typical of credit unions, potentially offering lower fees and better rates
Offers financial counseling, financial education, and student scholarships to support members
14th largest credit union in Maine with $318.1M in assets providing adequate capital strength
cons: Single branch location in Mexico, Maine limits physical accessibility for members outside immediate area
Geographic membership restrictions require living, working, worshiping, attending school, or conducting business in specific nine-county Maine region
Smaller institution (19,400 members) may offer less product customization or specialized services than larger regional competitors
Lobby currently closed according to website, indicating potential operational constraints or service limitations
Limited branch and ATM network (1 branch, 2 ATMs) compared to banks with broader geographic reach
best_for: Rural Maine residents in the nine-county service area seeking community-focused banking with personalized service
Small business owners and agricultural operations in Androscoggin, Cumberland, Franklin, Kennebec, Knox, Lincoln, Oxford, Sagadahoc, Somerset, or York counties
Members prioritizing credit union membership benefits, member-owned governance, and potential fee advantages over traditional banks
Customers seeking comprehensive digital banking (online and mobile) while maintaining a local community financial institution
diagnosis: Oxford Federal Credit Union is best for rural Maine residents and business operators within its nine-county service area seeking traditional credit union banking with community focus, digital convenience, and potential member-owner benefits. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership eligibility—applicants must live, work, worship, attend school, or conduct business in one of nine specific Maine counties, and the single-branch model limits physical accessibility outside the Mexico area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
